excerpts: All tests run in worker processes. These processes are OS processes, running independently, orchestrated by the test runner. All workers have identical environments and each starts its own browser.

From the command line:
```
Playwright Test runs tests in parallel. In order to achieve that, it runs several worker processes that run at the same time. By default, **test files** are run in parallel. Tests in a single file are run in order, in the same worker process.
npx playwright  test --workers 4
```

executive_summary.description: A high-level summary of the recommended E2E testing architecture, addressing the core problem of moving from a 'server-per-test-file' to a 'server-per-test-run' model using Vitest's global lifecycle hooks.

recommended_architecture_overview.description: A detailed overview of the proposed architecture. This involves using Vitest's `globalSetup` to spawn a single, long-lived server process for the entire test run, and leveraging a browser automation framework's features (like Playwright's Browser Contexts) to achieve test-level isolation against this shared server.

browser_lifecycle_strategy.description: Explanation of Playwright's 'one browser instance per test run' strategy for efficiency.

context_isolation_pattern.description: Details on how Playwright uses isolated browser contexts for each test file to prevent state leakage (cookies, storage).

page_and_fixture_scoping.description: Analysis of how test fixtures and pages are scoped to individual tests, ensuring a clean environment and reliable cleanup.

global_setup_role.description: Explanation of the `globalSetup` function's purpose: to spawn the shared server process before any tests run.

global_teardown_role.description: Explanation of the `globalTeardown` function's purpose: to gracefully terminate the server process after all tests complete.

state_communication_method.description: Describes the pattern for passing server details (like port or PID) from the global setup to individual test workers.

global_setup_script.description: Detailed instructions and a code example for the `globalSetup` file. It explains how to spawn the server as a child process, capture its PID, and establish a way to communicate its stdio handles or port number to the individual test files.

global_teardown_script.description: Detailed instructions and a code example for the `globalTeardown` function. It explains how to ensure the server subprocess is cleanly and reliably terminated using its PID, covering normal exit, `SIGTERM`, and cleanup even if tests crash.

communicating_server_details_to_tests.description: Describes strategies for exposing the shared server's details (like port number or socket path) from `globalSetup` to the test workers. This includes using a temporary file, environment variables, or returning a global state object from the setup function.

intra_fixture_isolation_best_practices.description: Best practices for ensuring test isolation within the shared server fixture. This covers strategies like using unique-per-test markers or identifiers for data, robust cleanup-on-failure logic (e.g., `try/finally` in tests or `afterEach` hooks), and coordination mechanisms for parallel tests.

test_strategy_unit_vs_e2e.description: Guidance on the appropriate split between unit tests and E2E tests for projects shipping a native app and a server. It outlines the purpose and scope of each test type in this context.

scope_of_unit_tests.description: Defines the ideal scope for unit tests: in-process, fast, focused on individual functions or modules with mocked boundaries (e.g., mocking the stdio protocol or native integrations).

scope_of_e2e_tests.description: Defines the ideal scope for E2E tests: testing the system as a whole with real processes, the real communication protocol, and interaction with the real target environment (e.g., the Safari browser).

anti_pattern.description: The name of the anti-pattern, e.g., 'Spawning a new server per test file'.

negative_impact.description: Explanation of why this practice is detrimental (e.g., slow test execution, resource-intensive, unrealistic test environment).

recommended_alternative.description: The best practice to use instead, e.g., 'Use a single, long-lived server process per test run'.

e2e_testing_anti_patterns.description: A list of known anti-patterns that make E2E tests brittle, slow, or diverge from production reality. Each item will detail a specific anti-pattern and why it should be avoided.

step_number.description: The sequential number of the step in the plan.

action.description: A concise title for the action to be taken in this step, e.g., 'Implement globalSetup File'.

details.description: A detailed description of the tasks involved in the step, including code snippets or configuration changes.

vitest_config_example.description: A concrete code snippet for the `vitest.config.ts` file, showing how to reference the `globalSetup` file.

title.description: The title of the referenced document or project.

url.description: The direct URL to the resource.

resource_type.description: The type of resource, e.g., 'Official Documentation', 'GitHub Repository', 'Blog Post', 'API Reference'.

relevance.description: A brief explanation of why this resource is relevant to the testing architecture.

documentation_and_oss_references.description: A curated list of links to official documentation (Playwright, Vitest) and mature open-source projects (e.g., Puppeteer, Node.js) that serve as concrete references for the recommended practices.
